retry_reason: kref_put is a release operation that always succeeds (or crashes if the pointer is NULL); it does not fail transiently. Retrying would cause a double-put bug.
contract_summary: This put wrapper releases a reference on an amdgpu_userq_fence_driver object. It decrements the reference count via kref_put, and when the count reaches zero, it calls the provided destroy callback (amdgpu_userq_fence_driver_destroy). After this call, the caller must not use the object any further. This function unconditionally releases a reference; it does not return any error. It is the counterpart to a get operation (e.g., amdgpu_userq_fence_driver_get). The caller must ensure that they do not double-put the same reference.

judge.reason: The function solely calls kref_put on the refcount field of the input structure, with no other logic. Its only purpose is reference-count release.
